title: hieracium norvegicum var. oxypetalum
intro: Hieracium norvegicum var. oxypetalum, commonly known as the saw-wort, is a perennial plant in the dandelion family that is native to Europe and Asia. The saw-wort plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of saw-wort include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for skin issues and respiratory problems. The saw-wort plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical ointment, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote wound healing and reduce the risk of infection.
